mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and strong underlying economic activity, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short-to-medium term.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rises above a critical threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance, increasing the cost of capital and potentially slowing economic growth, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60d returns in more than 60% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust retail sales (RSAFS) and stable, low inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates healthy consumer spending power without immediate pressure for aggressive monetary tightening, fostering a supportive environment for equity valuations and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in over 50% of periods where RSAFS growth is positive and CPIAUCSL annual growth is below 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of historically low unemployment (UNRATE), any significant uptick in the unemployment rate signals a potential inflection point in the business cycle, indicating an impending economic slowdown that negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during late-cycle expansions where UNRATE is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating robust demand in the housing sector that typically spills over into broader economic activity and supports positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where HOUST 3-month average growth exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal weakening manufacturing activity and overall economic contraction, which directly impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal from INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated and the Federal Reserve is actively raising interest rates (FEDFUNDS), it creates a challenging environment for equities due to higher discount rates and increased recession fears, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both inflation is high and rates are rising as per the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust GDP growth (GDPC1) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a strong and expanding economy, which provides a solid foundation for corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where both GDP growth is strong (e.g., >2% YoY) and unemployment is falling.
